expectations.text: Response acknowledges that no roadmap exists yet
expectations.evidence: Line 7: 'Current State: Fresh Project, No Roadmap Yet' and line 9: 'There is no docs/ROADMAP.md yet, and no PRDs have been written.'

expectations.text: Response summarizes the current codebase state or architecture
expectations.evidence: Lines 14-40: Detailed table of all components (App.tsx, ProcessFlow.tsx, AdminPanel.tsx, etc.) with line counts, status, and descriptions. Lines 31-40 list core capabilities already working (Google OAuth, CRUD, ReactFlow, AI features, etc.).

expectations.text: Response asks the user what they want to work on next
expectations.evidence: Lines 93-101: '## What Do You Want to Work On?' section explicitly asks the user to choose between starting a new feature, picking from the suggested list, or something else. Ends with 'What sounds right?'

expectations.text: Response does NOT invent features or progress that do not exist
expectations.evidence: The response accurately describes the codebase state and gaps. It identifies real issues (BpmnNodes.tsx is 25 lines, no test suite, no CI/CD, CLAUDE.md still has template tokens). The 'Recommended Next Steps' are suggestions, not claimed as existing progress.
